rulings.Case Title: SCOTT FRIEDMAN ET AL VS. MICHAEL CARBOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday February 22, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF SCOTT FRIEDMAN, REBECCA KADEN Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To File Second Amended Complaint GRANTED. The Court previously denied the motion for leave to amend expressly without prejudice providing Plaintiff with opportunity to address the issues as listed in the order. Plaintiff has done so and may file an amended complaint. Davis-Sterling Act does not apply to the newly proposed causes of action per CC 5930(b). The Court does not make any ruling as to whether ADR compliance is required regarding the newly added defendant as to previously asserted causes of action. If Defendant wishes to address this matter (based on good faith and authority) it may be addressed on a demurrer after good faith attempt to meet and confer. The Court notes that opposition was filed late without any explanation. However, since it was fully addressed in the reply, opposition was considered by the Court. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday February 22, 2019 line 3. Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Non-opposition filed. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for Quiet Enjoyment for Plaintiff to allege a contract-based (as opposed to tort-based) cause of action and seek corresponding damages as to the unit she currently occupies (432) as asserted in opposition. Demurrer to cause of action of action for UCL violation is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts constituting unfair, unlawful, or fraudulent practices within the meaning of UCL and with requisite specificity. Plaintiff is also to allege a remedy available under this statute. Demurrer to causes of action for breach of contract and covenant of good faith and fair dealing is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a breach and an excuse for her own non-performance under the contract. Plaintiff's interpretation of the contact clauses for landlord to provide possession of the new unit and the tenant to surrender possession of the old unit as dependent clauses is plausible and must be accepted as true on a demurrer. Demurrer based on misjoinder is overruled. Section 23 of the Lease does not release the former owner from liability for the wrongs/breaches committed prior to the transfer. =(501/REQ)
